Entailment Free-Choice (Study 1)

Bartosz

Overview

  • Task: judge whether a Conclusion follows from a Premise (keys: B = follows, N = does not follow).
  • Design: 8 practice trials (controls with feedback) + 96 experimental trials (48 critical modal/want; 48 control fillers; no feedback).
  • Stimuli metadata fields in results.csv: domain (modal, want, control), pair_type (e.g., allowed_and, want_single), conclusion_form (A, B, AND, or empty for controls), and validity (STIMULUS_VALID: Y, N, or ? for unknown/critical).

Data Preparation

Data summary: Data include 40 participant(s) with 320 practice trials and 3840 experimental trials. Session counts: practice = 320, experimental = 3840. Responses recoded to binary (B=1, N=0) and accuracy computed where validity was known.

Stimuli inventory (paper methods)

Manipulation Checks

Manipulation check: Participants contributed between 96 and 96 experimental trials (mean = 96). Trial counts cover all domains and pair types.

Paper-ready summary: want_single vs invalid controls

Primary contrast for the paper: the extent to which participants accept the FC-style inference from a want + disjunction premise to a single-disjunct want conclusion (want_single), compared to their tendency to accept clearly invalid control inferences.

Primary descriptives:

Group

Mean follows

SD

95% CI

N participants

Control: invalid

9.8%

10.5%

[6.4%, 13.2%]

40

Want: want_single

85.6%

30.4%

[75.9%, 95.4%]

40

Primary comparison test:

Paired t-test on participant means: t(39) = 14.82, p = <0.001, dz = 2.34.

Primary comparison (model-based): want_single vs invalid controls

In addition to participant-mean descriptives, we estimate the same contrast using a mixed-effects logistic regression on trial-level responses.

Model-based estimated “follows” rates:

paper_group

prob

SE

df

asymp.LCL

asymp.UCL

0.07436176

Want: want_single

0.91080635

0.05731949

Inf

0.71922519

0.97602366

Model-based comparison:

Mixed-effects logistic regression (random intercepts for participant and item): OR = 269.52, 95% CI [58.87, 1233.88], z = 7.21, p = <0.001.

Additional comparisons: benchmark modal FC vs want FC

The main text currently reports the primary contrast (want_single vs invalid controls). For interpretability, it is also useful to compare FC endorsement rates in benchmark permission modals (allowed) to FC endorsement rates under want.

We focus on the four directly comparable conditions:

  • Modal (allowed): allowed_single and allowed_and
  • Want: want_single and want_and
